titleStmt.title: Letter from G. W. Scroope to William Dawson, 7 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: G. W. Scroope

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

correspAction.placeName: The Central Criminal Lunatic Asylum, Dundrum, Co. Dublin

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
1863 OFFICE OF LUNATIC ASYLUM
DUBLIN CASTLE
REC
8 MAR 1916
All communications
to be addressed to-
The Governor.
The Central Asylum,
Dundrum,
Co. Dublin. In any further correspondence on
this subject, the following number
must be quoted.
468 / 16
Subject:- The Central Criminal Lunatic Asylum,
DUNDRUM,
Co. DUBLIN, 7th March 1916 Gentlemen, I beg to acknowledge the receipt of your letter of
the 3rd instant, No 1858, forwarding a copy of Treasury Letter
No 845/16, dated 24th ult., approving of the Estimate of
£8740 for the Asylum for the year 1916-17. With regard to the second paragraph of the letter
I beg to state that I consider that it would be inadvisable
to substitute margarine for butter in the dietary for att-
endants for the following reasons:- No1. The present dietary given to attendants for
breakfast and tea rations is in my opinions a very unsatisfact-
ory one, and I believe compares unfavorable with Broadmoor
Asylum. The allowances are as follows:-
1/4 pt Milk
5/16th oz Tea
8/7th oz Sugar
1/2 lb Bread
27/28 oz Butter. No2. I have no doubt that the substitution of
margarine To The Inspectors
of Lunatics
Dublin Castle.
margarine for butter would cause a great amount of dissatisf-
action, as it is the general opinion of attendants that their
breakfast ration should be substantially improved. No. 3. The number of attendants in this asylum is
small as compared with Broadmoor Asylum, and no appreciable
gain would be effected. In fact I estimate that in six months time, the
saving would only be about £30. I am,
Gentlemen,
Your obedient Servant
G. W. Scroope
Acting Governor.
